content.question: Will the Fidesz opposition signal any willingness to negotiate a cross-party consensus on selection reform, or will it maintain the 'totalitarian/communism' framing and signal intent to reverse the reform on return to government?
content.reduction_path: Public statements by Fidesz leadership (e.g., via opposition spokesperson or shadow minister) indicating willingness to negotiate or conditions for consensus; polling showing public support for selection reform high enough to make opposition costly; evidence of behind-the-scenes dialogue or negotiation frameworks.
